repos.description: 🪨 why use many token when few token do trick — Claude Code skill that cuts 65% of tokens by talking like caveman
repos.blurb: Caveman is a Claude Code skill that forces the AI to communicate using simplified "caveman-speak" syntax. It reduces token usage by approximately 75% while maintaining technical accuracy, making responses faster and more cost-effective. The approach is backed by research showing that brevity constraints can actually improve LLM accuracy by 26 percentage points on certain benchmarks.

repos.description: A collection of DESIGN.md files inspired by popular brand design systems. Drop one into your project and let coding agents generate a matching UI.
repos.blurb: VoltAgent/awesome-design-md is a curated collection of DESIGN.md files that capture the design systems of popular websites in a standardized markdown format. Developers can drop these files into their projects and use them with AI coding agents to generate UI components that match the visual style of sites like Claude, Cohere, or ElevenLabs. The DESIGN.md format follows Google Stitch specifications and includes detailed sections covering color palettes, typography, components, and layout principles that LLMs can easily parse.

repos.description: An agentic skills framework & software development methodology that works.
repos.blurb: Superpowers is an agentic skills framework that provides a structured software development workflow for coding agents through composable skills and initial instructions. It enforces a disciplined process where agents gather requirements, create validated designs, generate detailed implementation plans, and execute work through subagent-driven development rather than jumping straight into coding. The framework supports multiple AI coding platforms including Claude Code, Cursor, and others through plugin marketplaces or manual installation.

repos.description: The agent harness performance optimization system. Skills, instincts, memory, security, and research-first development for Claude Code, Codex, Opencode, Cursor and beyond.
repos.blurb: Everything Claude Code is a performance optimization system for AI agent harnesses, providing skills, instincts, memory, and security capabilities for Claude Code, Codex, and similar tools. It offers production-ready configurations, hooks, and MCP setups developed through 10+ months of intensive daily use building real products, focusing on token optimization, memory persistence, and security scanning. The project won an Anthropic hackathon and has accumulated over 100k GitHub stars.

repos.description: AI coding assistant skill (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot CLI, OpenClaw, Factory Droid, Trae, Google Antigravity). Turn any folder of code, docs, papers, images, or videos into a queryable knowledge graph
repos.blurb: Graphify is an AI coding assistant skill that converts folders of code, documentation, papers, or images into queryable knowledge graphs. It helps developers understand codebases faster by extracting and visualizing relationships between components, architectural decisions, and design rationale across multiple file types. The tool uses a two-pass approach combining deterministic AST parsing with Claude-powered concept extraction, requiring 71.5x fewer tokens per query compared to reading raw files.

repos.description: The best-benchmarked open-source AI memory system. And it's free.
repos.blurb: MemPalace is a Python-based AI memory system that stores complete conversation history locally using ChromaDB for semantic search and retrieval. It achieved a 96.6% score on the LongMemEval benchmark by storing raw verbatim exchanges without summarization, allowing users to preserve and search through all AI interactions rather than losing context between sessions. The system includes an experimental compression dialect called AAAK, though it currently performs worse than the raw storage mode.

repos.description: AI-powered job search system built on Claude Code. 14 skill modes, Go dashboard, PDF generation, batch processing.
repos.blurb: Career-Ops is an AI-powered job search automation system built on Claude Code that provides structured evaluation, tracking, and application generation for software engineering roles. It automatically scores job opportunities using a weighted A-F system across 10 dimensions, generates ATS-optimized PDFs tailored to specific job descriptions, and scrapes job portals in batch to maintain a centralized pipeline dashboard. The system is designed to be customized by Claude itself, allowing users to modify scoring criteria, company lists, and evaluation frameworks through natural language requests.

repos.description: Your own personal AI assistant. Any OS. Any Platform. The lobster way. 🦞
repos.blurb: OpenClaw is a self-hosted personal AI assistant built in TypeScript that runs on your own devices. It integrates with existing communication channels like WhatsApp, Slack, Discord, and others while supporting voice interaction and a controllable canvas interface. The project emphasizes local deployment and data ownership, positioning itself as an alternative to cloud-based AI assistants.

repos.description: 你想蒸馏的下一个员工，何必是同事。蒸馏任何人的思维方式——心智模型、决策启发式、表达DNA。Distill how anyone thinks.
repos.blurb: nuwa-skill is a framework that distills the thinking patterns and cognitive models of notable figures like Steve Jobs, Elon Musk, and Paul Graham into reusable AI skills. It extracts six layers of cognition including mental models, decision heuristics, and communication styles to help users analyze problems through the lens of these influential thinkers. The system generates independent, installable skills for each person that can be directly invoked in Claude Code for domain-specific advice and analysis.

repos.description: The repo is finally unlocked. enjoy the party! The fastest repo in history to surpass 100K stars ⭐. Join Discord: https://discord.gg/5TUQKqFWd Built in Rust using oh-my-codex.
repos.blurb: Claw Code is a Rust-based development harness and tooling framework that claims to be a clean-room rewrite of leaked proprietary code. It provides an agent runtime with API client abstraction, tool execution framework, plugin system, and CLI for development workflow automation. The project gained notable attention by reaching 50K GitHub stars within 2 hours of publication.

repos.description: The open source coding agent.
repos.blurb: OpenCode is an open source AI coding agent written in TypeScript that helps developers write, modify, and manage code through natural language interactions. It provides both command-line and desktop interfaces for automated code generation, refactoring, and project assistance across multiple programming languages. The project has gained significant traction with over 128k GitHub stars and offers extensive installation options across all major platforms and package managers.

repos.description: 数字生命卡兹克开源的 AI Skills 合集
repos.blurb: This is a collection of open-source AI Skills following the Agent Skills standard, designed to extend AI agent capabilities through structured instruction sets. It provides reusable, composable modules that agents can automatically load for specific scenarios or manually invoke via commands like `/skill-name`. Currently includes a single skill for Chinese blog writing with style rules, quality checks, and content methodology.

repos.description: Garry's Opinionated OpenClaw/Hermes Agent Brain
repos.blurb: GBrain is a personal knowledge management system that stores structured information about people, companies, and ideas as searchable markdown files backed by Postgres with vector search. It's designed to make AI agents progressively smarter by maintaining detailed dossiers and relationship histories that compound over time, enabling queries like "prep me for my meeting with Jordan" or "who should I invite to dinner who knows both Pedro and Diana." The system runs a "dream cycle" that automatically processes daily conversations overnight to enrich entities and consolidate memory while you sleep.

repos.description: Claude Code skill for generating production-quality SVG+PNG technical diagrams. Supports 8 diagram types, 5 visual styles, and deep AI/Agent domain knowledge.
repos.blurb: A Claude Code skill that generates production-quality technical diagrams from natural language descriptions, outputting both SVG and high-resolution PNG formats. It specializes in AI/Agent system architectures with built-in knowledge of patterns like RAG, multi-agent flows, and memory architectures, supporting 8 diagram types across 5 visual styles. The tool uses semantic shape vocabulary (hexagons for agents, cylinders for databases) and color-coded arrows to automatically convey technical relationships.

repos.description: Open Claude Is Open-source coding-agent CLI for OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama, Codex, GitHub Models, and 200+ models via OpenAI-compatible APIs.
repos.blurb: OpenClaude is a fork of the leaked Claude Code source that adds support for any OpenAI-compatible LLM instead of just Claude. It allows developers to use Claude Code's full toolset (bash, file operations, agents, MCP) with models like GPT-4o, DeepSeek, Gemini, or local models through Ollama. The project originated from Claude Code source maps that were accidentally exposed via npm in March 2026.

repos.description: 将冰冷的离别化为温暖的 Skill，欢迎加入数字生命1.0！Transforming cold farewells into warm skills? It's giving rebirth era. Welcome to Digital Life 1.0. 🫶
repos.blurb: This is a tongue-in-cheek Python project that generates AI skills to replicate departed colleagues' work patterns and personalities. It ingests workplace data (Feishu/DingTalk messages, documents, emails) along with personality descriptions to create AI personas that can write code in their style, answer questions in their voice, and even exhibit their blame-shifting tendencies. The project follows AgentSkills standards and includes automatic data collection from enterprise platforms plus conversation-based corrections to refine the generated personas.

repos.description: Linux kernel source tree
repos.blurb: The Linux kernel is the core operating system component that manages hardware resources and provides fundamental services for all other software in Linux distributions. It serves as the interface between applications and hardware, handling memory management, process scheduling, device drivers, and system calls. This is Linus Torvalds' original repository containing the complete kernel source tree used across countless systems from embedded devices to supercomputers.

repos.description: "OpenHarness: Open Agent Harness with a Built-in Personal Agent--Ohmo!"
repos.blurb: OpenHarness is an open-source Python implementation of an agent harness that provides the complete infrastructure (tools, memory, safety boundaries) to wrap around LLMs and make them functional agents. It offers 43+ tools and is designed for researchers to understand, experiment with, and extend production AI agent architectures. The project is 44x lighter than Claude Code while maintaining 98% tool compatibility and includes comprehensive testing with 114 unit tests.

repos.description: Use Codex from Claude Code to review code or delegate tasks.
repos.blurb: This is a Claude Code plugin that integrates OpenAI's Codex for code review and task delegation directly within the Claude Code editor. It provides commands for conducting standard or adversarial code reviews, delegating development tasks to Codex, and managing background jobs without leaving your existing workflow. The plugin supports both ChatGPT subscriptions and OpenAI API keys, with steerable adversarial reviews that can challenge design decisions and implementation choices.

repos.description: An opinionated list of Python frameworks, libraries, tools, and resources
repos.blurb: Awesome Python is a curated collection of Python frameworks, libraries, tools, and resources organized by category. It serves as a comprehensive reference for Python developers to discover quality packages across domains like web development, machine learning, data science, and DevOps tooling. With 288k+ stars, it's the 10th most-starred repository on GitHub.

repos.description: A list of Free Software network services and web applications which can be hosted on your own servers
repos.blurb: A curated list of free software network services and web applications that can be self-hosted on your own servers. It provides a comprehensive catalog of alternatives to SaaS providers across categories like communication, content management, file storage, and analytics for users who want to maintain control over their data and infrastructure. The repository has become the de facto standard reference with over 280k stars and maintains quality through automated link checking and project maintenance validation.

repos.description: Fair-code workflow automation platform with native AI capabilities. Combine visual building with custom code, self-host or cloud, 400+ integrations.
repos.blurb: n8n is a workflow automation platform that combines visual no-code building with custom JavaScript/Python capabilities and includes 400+ integrations plus native AI features. Technical teams use it to build complex automations while maintaining full control over their data through self-hosting or cloud deployment options. It operates under a fair-code license, making the source always visible while requiring enterprise licenses for commercial use at scale.

repos.description: 张雪峰.skill — 张雪峰的认知操作系统。高考志愿/考研/职业规划的实战思维框架。由女娲.skill生成。
repos.blurb: This is a conversational AI skill that simulates the cognitive framework and decision-making approach of Zhang Xuefeng, a prominent Chinese educational consultant known for pragmatic college and career advice. It provides guidance on academic choices, career planning, and life decisions using Zhang's signature analytical methods like "employment backward reasoning" and "social filter theory" rather than simply parroting his quotes. The skill was generated using the Nuwa.skill framework and is based on extensive research including 5 books, 15+ interviews, and 30+ direct quotes from Zhang Xuefeng.

repos.description: LLM Wiki is a cross-platform desktop application that turns your documents into an organized, interlinked knowledge base — automatically. Instead of traditional RAG (retrieve-and-answer from scratch every time), the LLM incrementally builds and maintains a persistent wiki from your sources。
repos.blurb: LLM Wiki is a cross-platform desktop application that automatically converts documents into an organized, interlinked knowledge base using LLM processing. Unlike traditional RAG systems that retrieve and process information for each query, it incrementally builds and maintains a persistent wiki structure that compiles knowledge once and keeps it current. The implementation is based on Andrej Karpathy's LLM Wiki methodology but extends it into a full desktop application with features like knowledge graphs, community detection, and web clipping.

repos.description: Production-ready platform for agentic workflow development.
repos.blurb: Dify is a production-ready platform for building and deploying AI agent workflows with visual low-code/no-code tooling. It provides orchestration capabilities for complex AI workflows including RAG, multi-agent systems, and integration with various LLM providers like OpenAI, GPT-4, and Gemini. The platform offers both cloud-hosted and self-hosted deployment options.

repos.description: 原汁原昧 Claude Code 可运行,可构建, 可调试版; Typescript 类型全修复; 企业级可靠性; 安全无毒, lock 文件保真, 可直接 bun i; bun run dev 启动
repos.blurb: This is a reverse-engineered TypeScript implementation of Anthropic's official Claude Code CLI tool, recreated from decompiled source code. It provides a full REPL interface with streaming conversations, tool execution capabilities (file operations, bash commands, web search, etc.), and support for multiple API backends including Anthropic Direct, AWS Bedrock, and Google Vertex. The project explicitly warns about its uncertain longevity and recommends downloading rather than forking due to rapid updates driven by Claude Opus optimization.

repos.description: The agent engineering platform
repos.blurb: LangChain is a Python framework for building AI agents and LLM-powered applications through composable, interoperable components. It provides standardized interfaces for models, vector stores, and third-party integrations to enable rapid prototyping while maintaining flexibility to swap models and data sources as requirements evolve. The framework is part of a broader ecosystem including LangGraph for agent orchestration and LangSmith for debugging and deployment.

repos.description: The official Lark/Feishu CLI tool, maintained by the larksuite team — built for humans and AI Agents. Covers core business domains including Messenger, Docs, Base, Sheets, Calendar, Mail, Tasks, Meetings, and more, with 200+ commands and 20+ AI Agent Skills.
repos.blurb: The `larksuite/cli` is a command-line tool for interacting with the Lark/Feishu Open Platform APIs. It provides over 200 commands covering business domains like messaging, documents, calendars, and spreadsheets, designed specifically for both human users and AI agents to automate workplace tasks. The tool features 19 structured AI Agent Skills and a three-layer command architecture that ranges from simple shortcuts to full API coverage.

repos.description: EmDash is a full-stack TypeScript CMS based on Astro; the spiritual successor to WordPress
repos.blurb: EmDash is a full-stack TypeScript CMS built on Astro and Cloudflare that reimagines WordPress with modern serverless architecture. It provides WordPress-like extensibility and admin UX while solving fundamental security issues by running plugins in sandboxed Worker isolates with declared capability manifests. Content is stored as structured JSON (Portable Text) rather than HTML, decoupling content from presentation for multi-platform rendering.

repos.description: Get up and running with Kimi-K2.5, GLM-5, MiniMax, DeepSeek, gpt-oss, Qwen, Gemma and other models.
repos.blurb: Ollama is a local runtime for running open-source language models like Llama, Gemma, Qwen, and others on your machine. It provides a simple CLI interface and REST API for downloading, running, and interacting with these models without requiring cloud services or API keys. The project uses llama.cpp as its backend and offers official Python and JavaScript libraries for integration.

repos.description: f.k.a. Awesome ChatGPT Prompts. Share, discover, and collect prompts from the community. Free and open source — self-host for your organization with complete privacy.
repos.blurb: prompts.chat is a curated collection of prompt examples designed for AI chat models like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. It serves as a community-driven library where users can discover, share, and collect effective prompts for various AI interactions, with self-hosting options for organizational privacy. The project has gained significant recognition with 143k+ GitHub stars and endorsements from AI industry leaders including OpenAI co-founders.

repos.description: Claude Code 泄露源码 - 本地可运行版本，新增跨平台桌面端软件补齐Computer Use（附带核心模块解析）
repos.blurb: cc-haha is a locally runnable version of Claude Code based on leaked source code that has been fixed and patched. It provides a complete Ink TUI interface and supports integration with any Anthropic-compatible API (like MiniMax, OpenRouter) for AI-assisted coding tasks. The project fixes multiple blocking issues from the original leaked source to make the full interactive terminal interface functional locally.

repos.description: A curated list of awesome Go frameworks, libraries and software
repos.blurb: This is a curated list of Go frameworks, libraries, and software packages organized by category. It serves as a comprehensive reference for Go developers to discover quality tools and libraries for their projects, similar to other "awesome" lists in the open source community. With 168,035 stars, it's one of the most popular resources in the Go ecosystem.

repos.description: freeCodeCamp.org's open-source codebase and curriculum. Learn math, programming, and computer science for free.
repos.blurb: freeCodeCamp is the open-source codebase and curriculum powering the freeCodeCamp.org learning platform for programming education. It provides a complete full-stack web development curriculum with interactive coding challenges, projects, and free certifications to help people transition into tech careers. The platform has helped over 100,000 people land their first developer job and operates as a donor-supported nonprofit.

repos.description: Auto generated skeleton loading framework
repos.blurb: Boneyard is a TypeScript framework that automatically generates pixel-perfect skeleton loading screens by extracting DOM element positions from your actual components. It eliminates the need to manually create placeholder layouts by using `getBoundingClientRect()` to capture real element dimensions and positions, then rendering matching gray rectangles during loading states. The framework includes responsive breakpoint support and generates skeletons via a CLI build step that snapshots your running application.

repos.description: Web UI consciousness monitor for Hermes — the AI agent with persistent memory
repos.blurb: Hermes HUD is a web-based dashboard that monitors the consciousness and memory state of Hermes AI agents. It provides real-time visibility into the agent's identity, memory usage, learned skills, active projects, and operational metrics through a browser interface that updates automatically via WebSocket connections. The system reads directly from the agent's data directory without requiring additional integrations and includes intelligent caching to minimize performance impact.

repos.description: Interactive roadmaps, guides and other educational content to help developers grow in their careers.
repos.blurb: Developer Roadmap is a community-driven collection of interactive learning paths and guides for software engineers across different technologies and career tracks. It provides structured curricula covering everything from frontend/backend development to DevOps, data science, and engineering management to help developers plan their skill development. The roadmaps feature interactive nodes that can be clicked for detailed explanations of each topic.

repos.description: 🤗 Transformers: the model-definition framework for state-of-the-art machine learning models in text, vision, audio, and multimodal models, for both inference and training.
repos.blurb: Transformers is a Python library that provides standardized implementations and pre-trained weights for state-of-the-art machine learning models across text, vision, audio, and multimodal domains. It enables developers to quickly deploy or fine-tune models like BERT, GPT, and vision transformers for both inference and training with minimal boilerplate code. The library integrates with PyTorch and TensorFlow and connects directly to Hugging Face's model hub for easy access to thousands of pre-trained models.

repos.description: Python - 100天从新手到大师
repos.blurb: This is a comprehensive Chinese-language Python tutorial structured as a 100-day curriculum covering everything from basic syntax to advanced topics like web development, data science, and machine learning. It provides a structured learning path for beginners to become proficient Python developers, with hands-on examples and practical projects throughout the course. The project has gained significant traction with over 180k stars and includes supplementary content on platforms like Bilibili for video learning.

repos.description: Display and control your Android device
repos.blurb: scrcpy is a cross-platform application that mirrors Android devices over USB or WiFi, allowing control via computer keyboard and mouse without requiring root or device-side apps. It provides low-latency screen mirroring with high performance (30-120fps) and additional features like audio forwarding, recording, and camera mirroring. The tool achieves 35-70ms latency and ~1 second startup time while being completely non-intrusive to the Android device.

repos.description: :books: Freely available programming books
repos.blurb: This is a curated list of freely available programming books and learning resources organized by language and topic. Developers use it to find quality educational materials without cost, covering everything from beginner tutorials to advanced technical references. With over 384k stars, it's one of GitHub's most popular repositories and includes a searchable web interface.

repos.description: All Algorithms implemented in Python
repos.blurb: TheAlgorithms/Python is a comprehensive collection of algorithm implementations written in Python for educational purposes. It serves as a learning resource for students, interview preparation, and algorithm competitions, covering everything from basic sorting and searching to advanced data structures. The implementations prioritize clarity over performance and may be less efficient than standard library equivalents.

repos.description: Visual Studio Code
repos.blurb: Visual Studio Code is an open-source code editor built on Electron that provides comprehensive editing, debugging, and extensibility features for software development. It combines the simplicity of a lightweight editor with integrated development tools like IntelliSense, Git integration, and a rich extension ecosystem. This repository contains the MIT-licensed "Code - OSS" source code, while the distributed Visual Studio Code product includes additional Microsoft-specific customizations under a proprietary license.

repos.description: Claude Code skills based on The Minimalist Entrepreneur by Sahil Lavingia
repos.blurb: This is a Claude Code plugin that implements business decision-making skills based on Sahil Lavingia's "The Minimalist Entrepreneur" methodology. It provides nine commands (like `/validate-idea`, `/mvp`, `/pricing`) that guide users through the entrepreneurial process from finding a community to scaling sustainably. The skills follow the book's structured progression from initial community building through product development to company culture decisions.

repos.description: Microsoft PowerToys is a collection of utilities that supercharge productivity and customization on Windows
repos.blurb: PowerToys is a Microsoft-maintained collection of over 25 Windows utilities that add missing system functionality and productivity features. It provides tools like window management (FancyZones), bulk file renaming (PowerRename), application launcher (PowerToys Run), and various system tweaks that Windows lacks natively. The project has significant community adoption with over 130k GitHub stars and active development from Microsoft.

repos.description: A self-hosted ML coding practice platform. 68 problems from ReLU to flow matching — attention, training, RLHF, diffusion, and more. Instant feedback in the browser.
repos.blurb: Pyre Code is a self-hosted platform for practicing machine learning implementation skills through 68 coding problems covering modern AI systems like Transformers, vLLM, and diffusion models. It provides instant feedback on submissions via a local grading service and runs entirely in the browser without requiring GPUs, making it ideal for interview preparation and deepening understanding of ML internals. The platform uses torch_judge for code execution and validation, ensuring submissions are tested against real PyTorch implementations.

repos.description: Langflow is a powerful tool for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows.
repos.blurb: Langflow is a Python platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows with a visual drag-and-drop interface. It allows developers to create LLM-based applications without extensive coding while providing source code access for customization, and can deploy workflows as APIs or export them for integration into existing applications. The platform supports multi-agent orchestration and includes built-in integrations with major LLMs, vector databases, and observability tools.

repos.description: Your favorite Terminal Coding Agent, now in Rust
repos.blurb: Claurst is a Rust reimplementation of Claude Code, Anthropic's AI coding CLI terminal agent, created after the original source code was leaked via an npm sourcemap. It provides the same AI-powered coding assistance and terminal integration as Claude Code but written in idiomatic Rust rather than TypeScript. The project was built using clean-room engineering principles, with separate AI agents handling specification and implementation phases to avoid copyright issues.

repos.description: 🙃   A delightful community-driven (with 2,400+ contributors) framework for managing your zsh configuration. Includes 300+ optional plugins (rails, git, macOS, hub, docker, homebrew, node, php, python, etc), 140+ themes to spice up your morning, and an auto-update tool that makes it easy to keep up with the latest updates from the community.
repos.blurb: Oh My Zsh is a community-driven framework for managing zsh shell configuration with over 300 plugins and 140 themes. It simplifies terminal customization and productivity enhancement through pre-built integrations for common development tools like git, docker, and various programming languages. The project has attracted 185k+ stars and contributions from 2,400+ developers, making it the de facto standard for zsh configuration management.

repos.description: [NOTE] Parent repo is migrating ownership, until the operation is done this repo is blocked. The free build of Claude Code. All telemetry removed, security-prompt guardrails stripped, all experimental features enabled.
repos.blurb: `free-code` is a modified build of Anthropic's Claude Code CLI tool with telemetry, security guardrails, and experimental feature gates removed. It provides a terminal-native AI coding agent that works with multiple model providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.) without phoning home or applying additional prompt-level restrictions beyond the model's inherent training. The project unlocks 54 experimental features that are disabled in the official release and eliminates all analytics/crash reporting functionality.

repos.description: Give Claude Code a memory that evolves with your codebase. Hooks automatically capture sessions, the Claude Agent SDK extracts key decisions and lessons, and an LLM compiler organizes everything into structured, cross-referenced knowledge articles - inspired by Karpathy's LLM Knowledge Base architecture.
repos.blurb: This is a Python tool that automatically captures conversations with Claude Code and compiles them into a structured, searchable knowledge base using the Claude Agent SDK. It extracts key decisions, lessons, and patterns from your coding sessions and organizes them into cross-referenced markdown articles that get injected back into future sessions. Uses simple index-based retrieval instead of RAG/vector databases, following Karpathy's architecture principle that structured indexes outperform similarity search at personal scale.

TurboQuant+ is a Python implementation of KV cache compression for transformer models that achieves 4.6x compression using PolarQuant with Walsh-Hadamard rotation. It enables memory-efficient local LLM inference with ~1% perplexity increase while maintaining near-baseline prefill speeds and introducing "Sparse V" attention-gated decoding that skips low-contribution computations for up to 22.8% decode speedup at long contexts. The implementation includes a C port integrated into llama.cpp with Metal GPU kernels and has been validated across multiple hardware configurations including Apple Silicon and NVIDIA GPUs.

repos.description: AutoGPT is the vision of accessible AI for everyone, to use and to build on. Our mission is to provide the tools, so that you can focus on what matters.
repos.blurb: AutoGPT is a platform for creating, deploying, and managing autonomous AI agents that can automate complex workflows. It provides both a low-code agent builder interface and pre-configured agents, allowing users to either customize their own automation workflows or deploy ready-made solutions. The platform can be self-hosted via Docker or accessed through their cloud-hosted beta service.

repos.description: Java 面试 & 后端通用面试指南，覆盖计算机基础、数据库、分布式、高并发、系统设计与 AI 应用开发
repos.blurb: JavaGuide is a comprehensive Chinese-language interview preparation guide for Java backend developers, covering computer science fundamentals, databases, distributed systems, concurrency, and system design. It serves as a study resource for backend engineering interviews, providing structured learning paths, practice questions, and detailed explanations of core concepts. With over 154k GitHub stars, it has become one of the most popular Java interview preparation resources in the Chinese developer community.

repos.description: The library for web and native user interfaces.
repos.blurb: React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces through a component-based, declarative approach. It enables developers to create interactive UIs by composing reusable components that efficiently update when data changes, and can be adopted gradually into existing projects. The library uses JSX syntax and supports both web and mobile development through React Native.

This is a reverse-engineered TypeScript reconstruction of Anthropic's Claude Code CLI tool, extracted from source maps in the published npm package `@anthropic-ai/claude-code`. It provides insight into the internal structure and implementation of Claude's code analysis capabilities, including tools for file editing, git operations, and multi-agent coordination. The repository contains 4,756 restored files from version 2.1.88, created purely for research purposes.

repos.description: A local dashboard for tracking your Claude Code token usage, costs, and session history. Pro and Max subscribers get a progress bar. This gives you the full picture.
repos.blurb: Claude Code Usage Dashboard is a local Python tool that parses Claude Code's JSONL usage logs to provide token usage tracking and cost estimates. It fills visibility gaps in Anthropic's official UI by showing detailed statistics, charts, and session history for API, Pro, and Max plan users across CLI, VS Code extension, and dispatched sessions. The tool requires zero dependencies beyond Python's standard library and uses incremental scanning to efficiently process only new or changed log files.

repos.description: The React Framework
repos.blurb: Next.js is a React framework for building full-stack web applications with server-side rendering, static site generation, and client-side routing capabilities. It provides an opinionated development environment that handles routing, bundling, and deployment optimizations out of the box, allowing developers to focus on application logic rather than configuration. The framework uses Rust-based tooling for faster builds and is widely adopted by enterprise applications.

repos.description: Fine-tune Gemma 4 and 3n with audio, images and text on Apple Silicon, using PyTorch and Metal Performance Shaders.
repos.blurb: Gemma Multimodal Fine-Tuner is a tool for fine-tuning Google's Gemma language models on text, image, and audio data using Apple Silicon Macs. It enables training multimodal LoRA adapters without requiring NVIDIA GPUs, and supports streaming large datasets from cloud storage (GCS/BigQuery) without filling local storage. The tool includes a real-time training visualizer that displays loss curves, attention heatmaps, and token predictions in a browser interface during training.

repos.description: A collection of useful .gitignore templates
repos.blurb: GitHub's official collection of .gitignore file templates for various programming languages, frameworks, tools, and environments. Developers use these templates to quickly set up proper file exclusion rules when creating new repositories, preventing unimportant files from being committed to version control. The templates are organized into three tiers: common use cases in the root, global editor/OS templates, and specialized community templates.

repos.description: An Open Source Machine Learning Framework for Everyone
repos.blurb: TensorFlow is an open-source machine learning framework that provides a comprehensive platform for building, training, and deploying ML models. It enables researchers to conduct cutting-edge ML research and allows developers to easily integrate machine learning capabilities into production applications through its flexible ecosystem of tools and libraries. The framework provides stable Python and C++ APIs along with GPU acceleration support for scalable model training and inference.

repos.description: Research on Coding Agents
repos.blurb: This is a research repository that analyzes the architecture of CLI coding agents, specifically focused on reverse-engineering Claude Code through publicly available information. It provides deep technical analysis including tool systems, permission flows, telemetry mechanisms, and internal features like "undercover mode" for hiding AI authorship. The project includes comprehensive multilingual documentation covering 40+ tools, progressive harness mechanisms, and undocumented features discovered through community research.

repos.description: Flutter makes it easy and fast to build beautiful apps for mobile and beyond
repos.blurb: Flutter is Google's open-source SDK for building cross-platform applications from a single Dart codebase that targets mobile, web, desktop, and embedded platforms. It enables developers to create native-performance apps across multiple platforms without maintaining separate codebases, using hardware-accelerated rendering through Skia and Impeller graphics libraries. The framework provides pixel-level control over UI rendering and includes comprehensive widget libraries for both Material Design and Cupertino styling.

repos.description: TypeScript multi-agent orchestration engine — one runTeam() call from goal to result. Multi-model teams, auto task decomposition, parallel execution. 3 runtime dependencies.
repos.blurb: Open Multi-Agent is a TypeScript framework for orchestrating teams of AI agents that collaborate on complex tasks through automatic task scheduling and inter-agent communication. It enables building specialized agent teams where different agents handle planning, implementation, and review while the framework manages dependencies and parallel execution automatically. The framework is model-agnostic, allowing mixed teams of Claude and GPT agents running in a single Node.js process without subprocess overhead.

repos.description: Gemma Gem runs Google's Gemma 4 model entirely on-device via WebGPU — no API keys, no cloud, no data leaving your machine.
repos.blurb: Gemma Gem is a Chrome extension that runs Google's Gemma 4 language model locally in the browser using WebGPU. It provides an AI assistant that can interact with web pages by reading content, clicking elements, filling forms, and executing JavaScript without sending any data to external servers. The extension uses an offscreen document architecture to handle model inference while content scripts manage DOM manipulation tools.

repos.description: AI Agent 源码深度研究报告
repos.blurb: This is a research repository containing a deep dive analysis of Claude Code's source code and architecture, focusing on how modern coding agents are built. The analysis examines system prompts, agent orchestration, tools, plugins, hooks, and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to understand why these agents are more stable and effective than simple LLM+tool combinations. The repository provides a comprehensive PDF report but notably excludes the actual source code, focusing instead on educational analysis and commentary.

repos.description: The Go programming language
repos.blurb: This is the official repository for the Go programming language implementation and standard library. Go is designed for building simple, reliable, and efficient software with fast compilation and built-in concurrency primitives. The repository contains the complete language toolchain including compiler, runtime, and standard library used by millions of developers.

repos.description: A framework for building native applications using React
repos.blurb: React Native is a cross-platform mobile development framework that allows developers to build native iOS and Android applications using React and JavaScript. It provides access to native UI controls and platform APIs while enabling code reuse across platforms, making it faster to develop mobile apps compared to writing separate native codebases. The framework uses a bridge architecture written in C++ to communicate between JavaScript and native platform code.

repos.description: The most popular HTML, CSS, and JavaScript framework for developing responsive, mobile first projects on the web.
repos.blurb: Bootstrap is a comprehensive HTML, CSS, and JavaScript framework for building responsive, mobile-first web applications. It provides a standardized set of UI components, grid system, and utilities that accelerate front-end development and ensure consistent styling across projects. With over 174k GitHub stars, it's the de facto standard CSS framework in the web development ecosystem.

repos.description: Join Discord: https://discord.gg/5TUQKqFWd /  claw-code Rust port parity work - it is temporary work while claw-code repo is doing migration
repos.blurb: This is a temporary Rust port of the claw-code project while the original repository undergoes migration. It serves as a drop-in replacement during the transition period, providing continuity for users who depend on the claw-code functionality. The repository appears to be part of a broader effort to rewrite agent harness tools, with the Rust implementation targeting improved performance and memory safety.

repos.description: 《Hello 算法》：动画图解、一键运行的数据结构与算法教程。支持简中、繁中、English、日本語，提供 Python, Java, C++, C, C#, JS, Go, Swift, Rust, Ruby, Kotlin, TS, Dart 等代码实现
repos.blurb: Hello-algo is an open-source data structures and algorithms tutorial that uses animated visualizations and runnable code examples. It's designed for beginners to learn DSA concepts through visual explanations and hands-on practice, with implementations available in 13+ programming languages. The project supports four languages (Chinese Simplified/Traditional, English, Japanese) and has accumulated over 120k GitHub stars.

repos.description: :electron: Build cross-platform desktop apps with JavaScript, HTML, and CSS
repos.blurb: Electron is a framework that enables building cross-platform desktop applications using web technologies (JavaScript, HTML, and CSS) by combining Node.js and Chromium. It allows web developers to create native desktop apps without learning platform-specific languages, powering applications like Visual Studio Code. The framework provides prebuilt binaries for macOS, Windows, and Linux across multiple architectures.

repos.description: Node.js JavaScript runtime ✨🐢🚀✨
repos.blurb: Node.js is an open-source, cross-platform JavaScript runtime environment that executes JavaScript code outside of web browsers. It enables server-side JavaScript development and is commonly used for building web servers, APIs, and command-line tools. The project follows a predictable release schedule with new major versions every 6 months and LTS (Long Term Support) versions for production stability.

repos.description: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal, understands your codebase, and helps you code faster by executing routine tasks, explaining complex code, and handling git workflows - all through natural language commands.
repos.blurb: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that runs in your terminal and understands your codebase to execute tasks through natural language commands. It handles routine coding tasks, code explanation, and git workflows, allowing developers to work faster without context switching between tools. The tool integrates with terminals, IDEs, and GitHub through @claude mentions.

repos.description: Collection of publicly available IPTV channels from all over the world
repos.blurb: A curated collection of publicly available IPTV channel streams from around the world, organized into M3U playlists. It provides direct streaming links that can be loaded into any compatible video player for watching live television channels over the internet. The repository explicitly contains only links to streams, not the video content itself, with over 113k stars indicating significant community adoption.

repos.description: See where your AI coding tokens go. Interactive TUI dashboard for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor cost observability.
repos.blurb: CodeBurn is a terminal-based dashboard that tracks token usage and costs across AI coding tools like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot. It helps developers monitor where their AI coding budget is being spent by analyzing session data, tracking success rates, and identifying inefficient usage patterns. The tool works by directly reading session data from disk without requiring API keys or proxies, and includes a native macOS menubar app.

repos.description: A feed-forward 3D foundation model for reconstructing scenes from streaming data
repos.blurb: LingBot-Map is a feed-forward 3D foundation model that reconstructs scenes from streaming image/video data using a Geometric Context Transformer architecture. It enables real-time 3D reconstruction at ~20 FPS with superior accuracy compared to existing streaming and optimization-based methods, making it suitable for applications requiring live scene understanding. The model uses paged KV cache attention for efficient processing of long sequences exceeding 10,000 frames without drift accumulation.

repos.description: A terminal emulator for the web
repos.blurb: wterm is a web-based terminal emulator with a Zig core compiled to WebAssembly and DOM rendering. It provides native browser features like text selection, clipboard support, and accessibility while maintaining near-native performance through a lightweight WASM binary. The core terminal logic compiles to just ~12KB and includes full VT100/VT220/xterm escape sequence support with features like alternate screen buffers and 24-bit color.

repos.description: The Red Sun vulnerability repository
repos.blurb: RedSun is a proof-of-concept exploit that abuses Windows Defender's cloud tag handling behavior. It leverages Defender's file rewriting mechanism to overwrite system files and escalate privileges when the antivirus detects a malicious file with a cloud tag. The vulnerability exploits the counterintuitive behavior where Windows Defender rewrites detected malicious files back to their original location instead of removing them.

repos.description: 全能协议分析工具：浏览器抓包 + MITM 代理 + 指纹伪装 + AI 分析 + MCP Server 无缝对接 AI Agent/IDE   |  All-in-one protocol analysis toolkit — built-in browser capture, MITM proxy, JS hooks, fingerprint spoofing, AI analysis & MCP server for agent integration
repos.blurb: Anything Analyzer is an Electron desktop application that captures web traffic and browser behavior using Chrome DevTools Protocol, then uses AI to analyze the captured data for protocol reverse engineering. It provides comprehensive network interception (HTTP, WebSocket, SSE), JavaScript hooking (fetch, XHR, crypto APIs), and storage monitoring to generate detailed protocol analysis reports. The tool includes an embedded multi-tab browser with automatic popup capture, making it particularly useful for analyzing complex authentication flows like OAuth.

video-use is an open-source Python tool that enables AI-powered video editing through Claude Code conversations. It automatically cuts filler words, applies color grading, burns subtitles, and generates animations by feeding the LLM text transcripts with word-level timestamps instead of raw video frames. The system uses a self-evaluation loop that checks rendered output quality at every cut boundary before presenting the final result.

repos.description: HTML PPT Studio — AgentSkill with 24 themes, 31 layouts, 20+ animations for building professional HTML presentations
repos.blurb: HTML PPT Studio is an AgentSkill for generating professional HTML presentations with 36 themes, 31 layouts, and 47 animations using pure static HTML/CSS/JS. It enables AI agents to create presentation decks from natural language prompts like "make a 8-page tech presentation with cyberpunk theme" without requiring any build tools or compilation steps. The project includes 14 full-deck templates extracted from real-world presentations and supports both CSS animations and canvas-based effects.

repos.description: 达尔文.skill —— 一个让你的Skill无限进化的系统：评估→改进→测试→保留或回滚 | Autoresearch-inspired autonomous skill optimization for Claude Code. Evaluate, improve, test, keep or revert.
repos.blurb: Darwin-skill is an autonomous optimization system for Agent Skills that continuously evaluates, improves, tests, and either keeps or reverts changes using a ratchet mechanism that only preserves measurable improvements. It addresses the challenge of maintaining large skill collections (60+ skills) by providing both structural analysis and actual performance testing with an 8-dimensional scoring system, rather than just format validation. Inspired by Karpathy's autoresearch, it implements a human-in-the-loop approach where the system autonomously optimizes individual SKILL.md files but pauses between phases for user confirmation.

repos.description: Extract any website's complete design system with one command. DTCG tokens, semantic+primitive+composite, MCP server for Claude Code/Cursor/Windsurf, multi-platform emitters (iOS SwiftUI, Android Compose, Flutter, WordPress), Tailwind v4, Figma variables, shadcn/ui, CSS health audit, WCAG remediation, Chrome extension. MIT, Playwright, Node 20+.
repos.blurb: designlang is a CLI tool that reverse-engineers complete design systems from any website by extracting colors, typography, spacing, shadows, layout patterns, and responsive behavior. It generates 8 output files including Tailwind configs, design tokens, React themes, and an AI-optimized markdown summary that can be fed to LLMs to recreate the design. Unlike other design extraction tools, it captures layout architecture, responsive breakpoints, and interaction states rather than just basic colors and fonts.

repos.description: Bypass DPI with IP/TCP-Header manipulation
repos.blurb: SNI-Spoofing is a Python tool that manipulates IP and TCP headers to bypass Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) systems. It allows users to circumvent network censorship and filtering by spoofing Server Name Indication (SNI) fields in TLS handshakes. The project appears to target Persian-speaking users based on the Farsi text in the documentation.

repos.description: AI-powered daily intelligence for indie hackers and builders. 20 questions, 10+ sources, every morning.
repos.blurb: BuilderPulse is an AI-powered intelligence service that analyzes 300+ daily signals from platforms like Hacker News, GitHub, Product Hunt, and Reddit to identify building opportunities for indie hackers. It delivers actionable insights through daily reports that highlight trending topics, market gaps, and specific 2-hour project suggestions based on real user demand and discussion patterns. The service provides bilingual coverage (English/Chinese) and maintains a public archive of daily analyses accessible via GitHub.

repos.description: Android app manager with VPN integration. Manages groups of apps by freezing/unfreezing them based on VPN connection state.
repos.blurb: Anubis is an Android app manager that freezes and unfreezes groups of applications based on VPN connection state. It ensures apps only run with their intended network configuration by completely disabling them at the system level using `pm disable-user`, preventing any network detection or data transmission. Unlike sandbox solutions that isolate apps in work profiles, disabled apps cannot execute any code or inspect the shared network stack to detect VPN presence.

repos.description: A programming language for AI systems
repos.blurb: Weft is a Rust-based programming language designed specifically for building AI systems that integrate LLMs, humans, APIs, and infrastructure as first-class primitives. It provides durable execution with automatic state management, type-safe orchestration of multi-step workflows that can pause for human input and resume days later, and generates visual program graphs automatically. The language is in early development with an intentionally small but opinionated node catalog, positioning itself as a foundation for AI system development rather than a production-ready tool.

repos.description: Polymarket bot that buys "No" on all non-sports markets
repos.blurb: Nothing Ever Happens is a Python bot that automatically trades on Polymarket by buying "No" positions on standalone non-sports yes/no markets. It's designed for users who believe most dramatic events that people bet on won't actually happen, providing automated execution with safety controls and position tracking. The bot requires three specific environment variables to be set before it will execute real trades, otherwise it runs in paper trading mode.

repos.description: Self-healing browser harness that enables LLMs to complete any task.
Open-source GEO content production system with AI tasks, review workflow, and publishing.
repos.blurb: GEOFlow is an open-source content production system designed for GEO/SEO content operations, built with PHP and PostgreSQL. It automates the entire content pipeline from AI model configuration and asset management to task scheduling, draft review, and publishing, enabling automated content sites and internal content operations. The system supports OpenAI-compatible APIs and includes Docker deployment with built-in scheduling and worker processes.

repos.description: 冰冷的钱就这样流进我温暖的口袋-游资（UZI）Skills — 51位投资大佬帮你看盘 · 22维数据 × 180条量化规则 × 17种机构分析方法 · A股/港股/美股
repos.blurb: UZI-Skill is a Chinese stock analysis engine that uses AI to generate comprehensive investment reports for A-shares, Hong Kong stocks, and US stocks. It processes 22 data dimensions through 180 quantitative rules and 17 institutional analysis methods, with 51 different investment personalities (from Buffett to Chinese day traders) providing scored evaluations. The tool outputs Bloomberg-style HTML reports, social media graphics, and uses only free data sources with zero API keys required.

Clearwing is an autonomous vulnerability scanner and source-code hunter built on LangGraph that operates in two modes: network penetration testing with 63 tools and source code vulnerability analysis. It provides AI-driven security testing capabilities using publicly available models, offering an alternative to proprietary tools like Anthropic's Glasswing for automated vulnerability discovery and exploitation. The tool includes human approval guardrails for exploit attempts and generates comprehensive reports with explicit evidence levels from suspicion through patch validation.

repos.description: A Palantir-ish dashboard for family trip planning.
repos.blurb: A React-based dashboard that applies military/intelligence UI patterns to family trip coordination, tracking multiple families' routes, timelines, and logistics for shared vacations. It treats trip planning as an "operation" with convoy tracking, mission timelines, and command-center aesthetics instead of traditional calendar tools. The project is intentionally overengineered as a UI experiment that makes coordinating a weekend cabin trip feel like running a live ops room.

repos.description: Open source, cloud native, Postgres platform with copy-on-write branching and scale-to-zero
repos.blurb: Xata is an open-source, cloud-native platform for self-hosting multiple Postgres instances on Kubernetes with copy-on-write branching and scale-to-zero capabilities. It's designed for organizations that need internal Postgres-as-a-Service or efficient dev/testing environments where you can clone terabytes of data in seconds and automatically scale down idle instances. The platform runs on CloudNativePG and OpenEBS, providing production-grade features like high availability, automatic failover, and PITR backups out of the box.

repos.description: logo-generator-skill
repos.blurb: This is an SVG logo generator that creates professional logos with multiple design variants and high-end showcase presentations. It's designed for startups, developers, and product teams who need quality logos quickly without the traditional back-and-forth with designers. The tool generates 6+ logo variants per request and creates professional showcase images using 12 different background styles through Gemini AI integration.

repos.description: skill for explaining architecture
repos.blurb: A Cursor IDE plugin that adds a `/how` skill for generating architectural explanations of codebases at a senior engineer level. It automatically chooses between simple single-agent explanations or complex multi-agent exploration with parallel subagents that analyze different aspects before synthesizing findings into structured documentation. The plugin includes an optional critique mode that spawns independent critics across different models to identify architectural problems.

repos.description: Public BTC trading context since 2020.
repos.blurb: This is a public dataset containing nearly six years of real Bitcoin trading data from a single account, including 43k+ orders and 173k+ execution records from BitMEX. It provides researchers and traders with a complete, timestamped archive of actual trading decisions and outcomes across multiple market cycles, rather than selective narratives or curated performance stories. The dataset emphasizes decision-making under uncertainty and includes comprehensive execution ledgers, wallet histories, and position snapshots that were made public before outcomes were known.

repos.description: Learn LLM internals step by step - from tokenization to attention to inference optimization.
repos.blurb: This is an educational repository that explains the internal workings of Large Language Models through step-by-step tutorials covering tokenization, attention mechanisms, and inference optimization. It provides detailed mathematical explanations with numeric examples to help engineers understand core LLM concepts like Byte Pair Encoding, Q/K/V attention matrices, scaling factors, and causal masking. The content is delivered through both written blogs and video tutorials, making complex LLM internals accessible to practitioners.

repos.description: Animated border beam effect for React
repos.blurb: BorderBeam is a React component that creates an animated traveling glow effect around the border of any wrapped element. It provides configurable color schemes, sizes, and animation controls for adding visual polish to cards, buttons, and other UI components. The component automatically detects border radius and uses GPU-accelerated CSS animations with pointer-events disabled to avoid interfering with wrapped content.

repos.description: One brain, many harnesses. Portable .agent/ folder (memory + skills + protocols) that plugs into Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Hermes, or DIY Python — and keeps its knowledge when you switch.
repos.blurb: Agentic-stack is a portable AI agent framework that packages memory, skills, and protocols into a standardized `.agent/` folder structure. It allows developers to maintain consistent AI agent state and knowledge across different coding environments like Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and others without losing context when switching tools. The system includes an automated review protocol where the agent stages learning candidates nightly, but requires human approval before incorporating them into long-term semantic memory.

repos.description: Diagnose your Claude Code sessions
repos.blurb: Claude Doctor is a TypeScript CLI tool that analyzes Claude AI conversation transcripts stored in `~/.claude/` to identify behavioral anti-patterns and problematic usage signals. It detects issues like edit thrashing, error loops, correction-heavy sessions, and sentiment drift to help developers understand when their Claude interactions are ineffective. The tool can automatically generate ready-to-paste rules for CLAUDE.md files based on your actual conversation history patterns.

repos.description: Reference code for the Meta-Harness paper.
repos.blurb: Meta-Harness is a framework for automated search and optimization of task-specific model harnesses—the code that manages what to store, retrieve, and present to a base model during execution. It enables automatic improvement of the scaffolding around LLM agents by iteratively evolving components like memory systems and execution frameworks rather than manually engineering them. The framework includes reference implementations for text classification memory optimization and Terminal-Bench 2.0 scaffold evolution.

repos.description: Distributed DuckDB - dual execution and differential storage
repos.blurb: OpenDuck is an open-source implementation of MotherDuck's distributed DuckDB architecture, providing differential storage, hybrid execution, and transparent remote database access through a DuckDB extension. It allows queries to seamlessly span local and remote data sources, with the system automatically splitting execution plans between your machine and cloud workers while maintaining DuckDB's native catalog interface. The project uses a minimal gRPC protocol with Arrow IPC for data transfer, making it backend-agnostic and extensible.

repos.description: 微信收藏可视化 Claude Code Skill — 从加密 DB 到交互式 HTML 报告的端到端管线
🎓 Path to a free self-taught education in Computer Science!
repos.blurb: OSSU Computer Science is a curated curriculum that provides a complete undergraduate-level computer science education using free online courses from top universities like MIT, Harvard, and Princeton. It offers a structured, self-paced learning path for individuals seeking comprehensive CS fundamentals without enrolling in a formal degree program. The curriculum is organized into intro, core, advanced, and final project sections, designed to be completable in about 2 years with 20 hours of weekly study.

repos.description: Open-source Windows and Office activator featuring HWID, Ohook, TSforge, and Online KMS activation methods, along with advanced troubleshooting.
repos.blurb: Microsoft Activation Scripts (MAS) is an open-source tool that bypasses Windows and Office activation requirements using multiple methods including HWID, KMS, and Ohook techniques. It provides a way to activate Microsoft products without purchasing legitimate licenses, which violates Microsoft's terms of service but is widely used for testing or cost avoidance. The project has gained significant traction with over 172k GitHub stars and offers both PowerShell and traditional batch file execution methods.

repos.description: JavaScript Style Guide
repos.blurb: Airbnb's JavaScript Style Guide is a comprehensive set of coding conventions and best practices for writing JavaScript code. Teams and developers adopt it to maintain consistent code quality, readability, and to leverage modern ES6+ features across their JavaScript projects. With over 148k stars, it has become one of the most widely adopted JavaScript style guides in the industry, complete with accompanying ESLint configurations for automated enforcement.

Fresh Start is a GitHub repository with 6,370 stars but lacks a description or README content to indicate its purpose. Without documentation or visible code, it's impossible to determine what functionality this project provides or why developers would use it. The high star count suggests it may have had significant content that has since been removed or the repository may be archived.

repos.description: "OpenSpace: Make Your Agents: Smarter, Low-Cost, Self-Evolving" -- Community: https://open-space.cloud/
repos.blurb: OpenSpace is a self-evolving engine that plugs into existing AI agents (like Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw) to make them learn and improve from real-world usage. It enables agents to automatically fix broken skills, share successful patterns across a community network, and reduce token costs by 46% through reusing proven solutions instead of reasoning from scratch. The platform creates collective intelligence where one agent's improvements benefit all connected agents in the network.

repos.description: MOSS-TTS-Nano is an open-source multilingual tiny speech generation model from MOSI.AI and the OpenMOSS team. With only 0.1B parameters, it is designed for realtime speech generation, can run directly on CPU without a GPU, and keeps the deployment stack simple enough for local demos, web serving, and lightweight product integration.
repos.blurb: MOSS-TTS-Nano is a 0.1B parameter multilingual text-to-speech model that runs on CPU without requiring a GPU. It's designed for real-time speech generation with simple deployment for local demos, web serving, and lightweight product integration. The model uses an autoregressive Audio Tokenizer + LLM pipeline and outputs native 48 kHz, 2-channel audio.

repos.description: HY-World 2.0: A Multi-Modal World Model for Reconstructing, Generating, and Simulating 3D Worlds
repos.blurb: HY-World 2.0 is a multi-modal world model framework that generates and reconstructs 3D worlds from diverse inputs including text, single images, multi-view images, and videos. It enables synthesis of navigable 3D scenes from minimal input and performs 3D reconstruction through a unified feed-forward model called WorldMirror 2.0 that predicts depth, normals, camera parameters, and 3D Gaussian Splatting attributes in a single pass. The system uses a four-stage pipeline combining panorama generation, trajectory planning, world expansion, and world composition components.

repos.description: Let Skills Evolve Collectively with Agentic Evolver
repos.blurb: SkillClaw is a framework for collective skill evolution in multi-agent LLM systems that automatically distills reusable skills from real user interactions and shares them across agent clusters via cloud storage. It operates transparently through a proxy that intercepts agent API calls, records session data, and syncs evolved skills back to agents without requiring changes to existing workflows. The system offers two evolution backends: a fixed 3-stage LLM workflow and an autonomous OpenClaw agent that can directly manipulate skill files.

repos.description: Demo of tools of capabilities of Tony Stark.
repos.blurb: F.R.I.D.A.Y. is a Tony Stark-inspired AI voice assistant built with Python that combines a FastMCP server backend with a LiveKit voice agent frontend. It allows users to interact via voice with an LLM (Gemini 2.5 Flash) that can call various tools like web search, news retrieval, and system information through real-time communication between the voice pipeline and MCP server. The system uses a two-component architecture where the MCP server exposes tools over Server-Sent Events and the voice agent handles speech-to-text, LLM reasoning, and text-to-speech in a LiveKit room.

repos.description: Web dashboard for Hermes Agent — multi-platform AI chat, session management, scheduled jobs, usage analytics & channel configuration (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp)
repos.blurb: Hermes Web UI is a Vue-based web dashboard that provides centralized management for Hermes Agent, a multi-platform AI chatbot system. It enables configuration and monitoring of AI chat sessions across 8 platforms (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, etc.), usage analytics, scheduled jobs, and system settings through a single interface. The dashboard includes an integrated web terminal and automatically discovers available AI models from configured provider credentials.

repos.description: A self-hosted email client with an AI agent, running entirely on Cloudflare Workers
repos.blurb: Agentic Inbox is a self-hosted email client that runs entirely on Cloudflare Workers infrastructure, using Email Routing for message delivery, Durable Objects for per-mailbox SQLite storage, and R2 for attachments. It provides a full-featured web email interface with an integrated AI agent that can read messages, search conversations, and automatically draft replies using Cloudflare's Workers AI platform. The AI agent automatically generates draft replies for incoming emails but requires explicit user confirmation before sending.

repos.description: The knowledge compiler. Raw sources in, interlinked wiki out. Inspired by Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern.
repos.blurb: llm-wiki-compiler is a TypeScript CLI tool that converts raw sources (URLs, files, documents) into an interlinked markdown wiki using LLM-powered concept extraction. It addresses the limitation of RAG systems that re-discover knowledge at query time by compiling sources once into a persistent, browsable knowledge base where concepts get dedicated pages and relationships accumulate over time. The tool uses a two-phase pipeline with incremental compilation and hash-based change detection to avoid reprocessing unchanged sources.

repos.description: ACG Type Indicator - MBTI inspired anime persona quiz
repos.blurb: ACGTI is a TypeScript-based web application that implements an MBTI-inspired personality quiz for anime/manga (ACG) characters. Users answer situational questions to discover their anime personality archetype and get matched with specific characters from popular series like Evangelion, Detective Conan, and Genshin Impact. The application runs entirely in the browser with no backend, processing all calculations locally for privacy.

repos.description: 명령어 설치 한번으로 한달 KT 인터넷 요금을 무료로 쓸 수 있습니다. 귀찮은 공문 작성 필요 없습니다.
repos.blurb: This is a TypeScript CLI tool that automates KT (Korean ISP) internet speed testing and billing compensation claims. It continuously monitors internet speeds against SLA requirements and automatically submits compensation requests when speeds fall below contractual minimums, potentially reducing monthly internet bills to zero. The tool requires KT's official speed testing program and only works on macOS/Windows since KT doesn't support Linux.

repos.description: The easiest way to turn SVGs into interactive React 3D components
repos.blurb: 3dsvg is a TypeScript library that converts SVG files and text into interactive 3D React components using Three.js and React Three Fiber. It provides a declarative API for creating extruded 3D objects with configurable materials, animations, and lighting, plus includes a visual web editor for designing and exporting 3D scenes. The library supports 10 material presets (gold, glass, chrome, etc.), 7 animation types, and can export to multiple formats including PNG, video, and 3D model files.

repos.description: Turn any CLI into a JavaScript API
repos.blurb: cli-to-js is a TypeScript library that automatically converts command-line tools into typed JavaScript APIs by parsing their `--help` output. It enables programmatic interaction with any CLI tool through a structured interface where subcommands become methods and flags become typed options, eliminating the need to construct shell command strings manually. The library includes validation that catches invalid flag names and provides correction suggestions, making it particularly useful for AI agents that need to interact with system tools.

repos.description: The open source operating system for AI-powered businesses
repos.blurb: EvoNexus is a TypeScript-based multi-agent operating system that orchestrates 38 specialized AI agents for business operations and software development workflows. It provides automated daily routines including financial reporting, community monitoring, code reviews, and strategic planning while supporting multiple LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, etc.) through a unified interface. The system uses markdown-based agent definitions rather than code, making it extensible without requiring compilation or SDK knowledge.

repos.description: WebGPU physics engine based on the AVBD solver
repos.blurb: webphysics is an experimental WebGPU-based physics engine that implements rigid-body and soft-body simulation using the AVBD (Augmented Vertex Block Descent) solver. It provides GPU-accelerated physics simulation for web applications, enabling high-performance collision detection, constraint solving, and dynamics computation directly in the browser. The implementation follows the complete AVBD pipeline including broad-phase collision detection with LBVH, narrow-phase contact generation, and parallel constraint solving with body coloring.

repos.description: Package any component into a self-contained, isolated widget
repos.blurb: isolet is a TypeScript library that packages any component into a self-contained, isolated widget using shadow DOM. It supports multiple frameworks (React, Solid, Svelte, vanilla JS) and can output widgets as script tags, ESM imports, or CommonJS modules with scoped styles that don't leak. The CLI automatically inlines CSS assets as data URIs and resolves dependencies at build time for truly portable widgets.

repos.description: Karpathy-style LLM knowledge base Agent Skill for OpenClaw/Codex. Experimental — will iterate over time.
repos.blurb: This is an OpenClaw/Codex agent skill that builds persistent, cross-linked Markdown knowledge bases where LLMs compile and organize raw sources into a structured wiki format, inspired by Andrej Karpathy's approach. It replaces traditional RAG retrieval with a compounding knowledge system where each compile/ingest/query cycle makes the wiki richer, with the LLM handling all writing and cross-referencing while humans provide sources and feedback. The project includes Obsidian plugin integration and a local web viewer with audit capabilities for iterative knowledge base improvement.

repos.description: Heal your social media feeds
repos.blurb: Bouncer is a browser extension that uses AI to filter unwanted posts from Twitter/X feeds based on natural language filter definitions. It allows users to define filter topics in plain language (like "crypto" or "engagement bait") and automatically hides matching posts in real-time using various AI models. The extension supports both local on-device inference via WebGPU and cloud-based AI services, with multimodal capabilities to filter based on both text and images.
